Storage¶

Persistence layer for suite runs and results.

Base class¶

Abstract storage backend.

All storage implementations must subclass StorageBackend and implement its abstract methods. The base class also provides async context manager support so callers can write:

async with SQLiteStorage("llmeval.db") as storage:
    await storage.save_run(suite_run)
class StorageBackend[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Provider-agnostic interface for persisting and retrieving suite runs.

Concrete subclasses must implement all abstract methods. The default __aenter__ / __aexit__ implementations delegate to initialize() and close() respectively, so subclasses only need to implement those two lifecycle methods.

abstractmethod async initialize()[source]¶

Set up the backend (create tables, open connections, etc.).

Must be called before any read/write operation. Calling it more than once on the same instance must be idempotent.

Raises:

StorageError – If the backend cannot be initialised.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod async close()[source]¶

Release any resources held by the backend (connections, files).

Safe to call even if initialize() was never called.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod async save_run(suite_run)[source]¶

Persist suite_run, inserting or replacing any existing record.

Parameters:

suite_run (SuiteRun) – Completed (or in-progress) run to store.

Raises:

StorageError – If the write fails.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod async get_run(run_id)[source]¶

Fetch a single run by its UUID.

Parameters:

run_id (str) – The run_id to look up.

Returns:

The matching SuiteRun.

Raises:

StorageError – If run_id is not found or the read fails.

Return type:

SuiteRun

abstractmethod async list_runs(*, suite_name=None, limit=50, offset=0)[source]¶

Return runs ordered by started_at descending (most recent first).

Parameters:

  • suite_name (str | None) – When provided, only runs whose suite_name matches exactly are returned.

  • limit (int) – Maximum number of runs to return. Defaults to 50.

  • offset (int) – Number of runs to skip (for pagination). Defaults to 0.

Returns:

List of SuiteRun objects. Returns an empty list when no runs match.

Raises:

StorageError – If the read fails.

Return type:

list[SuiteRun]

abstractmethod async delete_run(run_id)[source]¶

Remove a run and all its results from storage.

Parameters:

run_id (str) – The run_id to delete.

Raises:

StorageError – If run_id is not found or the delete fails.

Return type:

None

SQLite backend¶

SQLite storage backend.

Uses aiosqlite for non-blocking I/O. A single persistent connection is held for the lifetime of the backend instance (opened in initialize(), closed in close()). This makes :memory: databases usable in tests and avoids connection-per-operation overhead in production.

Table layout¶

One table — suite_runs — stores both indexed metadata columns (for fast queries and sorting without deserialisation) and a full JSON blob so the complete SuiteRun round-trips without loss:

suite_runs
├── run_id        TEXT PK
├── suite_name    TEXT  (indexed)
├── suite_version TEXT
├── model         TEXT
├── judge_model   TEXT
├── started_at    TEXT  ISO-8601 UTC, indexed for sorted listing
├── completed_at  TEXT  nullable
├── total_tests   INTEGER
├── passed_tests  INTEGER
├── failed_tests  INTEGER
├── errored_tests INTEGER
└── run_json      TEXT  full model_dump_json() blob
class SQLiteStorage(db_path)[source]¶

Bases: StorageBackend

SQLite-backed storage for suite runs.

Parameters:

db_path (str | Path) – Filesystem path to the SQLite database file, or the special string ":memory:" for an in-process in-memory database (useful in tests). Relative paths are resolved from the current working directory.

Example:

async with SQLiteStorage("llmeval.db") as storage:
    await storage.save_run(suite_run)
    runs = await storage.list_runs(suite_name="My Suite")
async initialize()[source]¶

Open the database connection and create schema if absent.

Idempotent — calling more than once has no effect if a connection is already open.

Raises:

StorageError – If the database file cannot be opened or the schema cannot be created.

Return type:

None

async close()[source]¶

Close the database connection.

Safe to call when no connection is open.

Return type:

None

async save_run(suite_run)[source]¶

Insert or replace suite_run in the database.

Uses INSERT OR REPLACE so calling this a second time with the same run_id overwrites the existing record (useful when a run is persisted in-progress and again after scoring).

Parameters:

suite_run (SuiteRun) – Run to persist.

Raises:

StorageError – If the write fails.

Return type:

None

async get_run(run_id)[source]¶

Fetch a single run by UUID or unambiguous prefix.

A prefix is unambiguous when exactly one stored run ID starts with it.

Parameters:

run_id (str) – Full UUID or a unique prefix (minimum 8 hex characters recommended).

Returns:

Fully deserialised SuiteRun.

Raises:

StorageError – If run_id matches zero or more than one run, or if deserialisation fails.

Return type:

SuiteRun

async list_runs(*, suite_name=None, limit=50, offset=0)[source]¶

Return runs ordered by started_at descending (most recent first).

Parameters:

  • suite_name (str | None) – Exact-match filter on suite name. None returns all.

  • limit (int) – Maximum number of runs to return.

  • offset (int) – Rows to skip for pagination.

Returns:

List of deserialised SuiteRun.

Raises:

StorageError – If the query fails.

Return type:

list[SuiteRun]

async delete_run(run_id)[source]¶

Delete a run from storage.

Parameters:

run_id (str) – UUID of the run to remove.

Raises:

StorageError – If run_id is not found or the delete fails.

Return type:

None
